title: Holmskioldia spinescens
taxonomy: Holmskioldia spinescens is a species within the family Polemoniaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the phlox or Jacob's-ladder family. This family comprises around 20-25 genera and is native to the Americas, Asia, and Africa. The genus Holmskioldia itself is a part of this larger family and contains only one species, which is Holmskioldia spinescens. This plant is native to the Himalayan region and is known for its unique flowers and characteristics. The classification of Holmskioldia spinescens is further supported by its phylogenetic relationships within the family Polemoniaceae.

intro: Holmskioldia sanguinea (also known as Holmskioldia spinescens) is a flowering plant species native to the Himalayas and the mountainous regions of Asia, characterized by its showy, fragrant flowers and spiny stems. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, headache, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Holmskioldia sanguinea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are also commonly used as tonics and remedies for various health conditions.
